title: papaver lateritium subsp. monanthum
intro: Papaver lateritium subsp. monanthum, also known as the single-flowered poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver lateritium plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the poppy family. The plant contains various alkaloids, such as isoquinoline and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These alkaloids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties, which may provide relief for individuals suffering from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Papaver lateritium subsp. monanthum is typically used to make extracts and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems.
